commands.review_summary:
The PR routes exec approval `argPattern` matching through `compileSafeRegex`, adds focused `matchAllowlist` regression coverage for unsafe, invalid, and safe patterns, and adds a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. for the source path: current main passes hand-authored `argPattern` strings into `new RegExp` before testing joined argv, and the PR now includes focused tests for unsafe pattern rejection. I did not establish a live OpenClaw allowlist repro from the PR evidence.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blockers are contributor real behavior proof or maintainer proof override plus dirty-branch resolution, not a safe automated code repair task.

target.title: fix(security): add ReDoS guard to exec approval argPattern matching
target.body:
## Problem

`matchArgPattern` in `src/infra/exec-command-resolution.ts` (line 323) compiles user-provided `argPattern` strings via `new RegExp(argPattern)` without any ReDoS validation. While patterns auto-generated by `buildArgPatternFromArgv` are safe (they use literal escaping), legacy hand-authored patterns from the exec-approvals allowlist could contain nested repetition (e.g., `(a+)+$`) that causes catastrophic backtracking.

The codebase already has a safe-regex module at `src/security/safe-regex.ts` with `compileSafeRegex()` that detects nested repetition, but it was not wired into this call site.

## Fix

Replace `new RegExp(argPattern)` with `compileSafeRegex(argPattern)`. If the pattern fails validation (nested repetition or invalid regex), return `false` (no match) instead of compiling the dangerous pattern.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: exec-approval argPattern matching now rejects patterns with nested repetition via the existing compileSafeRegex module, preventing potential ReDoS from hand-authored allowlist patterns.
Real environment tested: local source checkout of openclaw/openclaw at commit 71b79f008d (current upstream/main) on Linux x86_64, Node v26.0.0, vitest 4.1.6.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: pnpm tsgo:core (type check); node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-command-resolution.test.ts (focused tests).
Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

Type check:
```
$ pnpm tsgo:core
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo
```
No errors.

Tests (15 tests):
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-command-resolution.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  15 passed (15)
```

Observed result after fix: all 15 tests pass. Type check is clean. The change replaces `new RegExp()` with the existing `compileSafeRegex()` from the same codebase, so the validation logic is already battle-tested.
What was not tested: crafted ReDoS pattern in a live exec-approvals allowlist (would require a running OpenClaw instance with a malicious allowlist entry). The `compileSafeRegex` function has its own test suite at `src/security/safe-regex.ts`.
target.author: sebtardif
